hero.subtitle: SSH, SFTP, AI agents, themes, and plugins — all in one workspace.

terminal.title: Terminal Workspace
terminal.desc: Recursive splits, drag-resize, magnify — tile panes your way.

ssh.title: SSH & SFTP
ssh.desc: Tree-based host manager with dual-pane file transfer.

themes.title: 71+ Themes
themes.desc: Dark, light, and custom — with a live theme editor.

crossPlatform.title: Cross-platform
crossPlatform.desc: Native on macOS, Windows, and Linux.

aiAgent.title: AI Agent
aiAgent.desc: Chat with Claude, GPT, Gemini, DeepSeek — right next to your terminal.

zeroConfig.title: Zero Config
zeroConfig.desc: Auto-hooks for all your AI coding tools.

sessionRestore.title: Session Restore
sessionRestore.desc: Pick up right where you left off after restart.

extensions.title: Extensions
extensions.desc: VS Code-style plugin system with a marketplace.

mcp.title: MCP Tooling
mcp.desc: Connect tool servers and let agents call them from chat.

island.title: Dynamic Island
island.desc: A floating status bar that surfaces real-time agent activity and approvals.

portForward.title: Port Forwarding
portForward.desc: Forward local and remote ports over SSH with one click.

snippets.title: Code Snippets
snippets.desc: Save, organize, and instantly paste frequently used commands.

cloudSync.title: Cloud Sync
cloudSync.desc: Sync hosts, snippets, and settings across all your devices.

step1.title: Download
step1.desc: Grab the installer for your platform.

step2.title: Open Workspace
step2.desc: Launch and start your first terminal.

step3.title: Connect SSH
step3.desc: Add hosts and manage remote servers.

step4.title: Chat with AI
step4.desc: Pick a provider and start a conversation.

platforms.question: Which platforms are supported?
platforms.answer: macOS (Apple Silicon & Intel), Windows, and Linux. Downloads are on GitHub Releases.

agents.question: What AI tools does it support?
agents.answer: Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI, Cursor, OpenCode, Droid, and more — all in one unified panel.

approval.question: Can I approve actions without switching context?
approval.answer: Yes. Permission requests expand in-place with Allow / Deny buttons. No context switching needed.

data.question: Does my data leave my machine?
data.answer: Local-first by design. Sessions, terminal state, and approvals stay on your machine.

setup.question: How does zero-config work?
setup.answer: On first launch, hooks are written into each supported tool's config. A local bridge handles the rest.

resources.question: Is it resource-heavy?
resources.answer: No. Lightweight when idle, surfaces what you need without constant interruption.

opensource.question: Is Termlnk open source?
opensource.answer: Yes. Source is public under PolyForm Noncommercial 1.0.0. Downloads on GitHub Releases.

changelog.description: Release notes are pulled from GitHub Releases so downloads, dates, and highlights stay aligned with the open-source repository.
changelog.emptyDescription: Published releases from GitHub will appear here automatically.
changelog.errorDescription: GitHub Releases could not be loaded right now. The repository release page is still available.
changelog.title: Changelog

footer.tagline: A modern, extensible smart terminal for developers.
